_comment: Claude Code permission config for the implementer sandbox. Mounted read-only. First line of defense; the container boundary and the Gauntlet back it up (defense in depth, D-10). MATCHER NOTE: Claude Code reliably matches a trailing-glob dir pattern (`src/**`) against new-file writes, but NOT fully-literal paths nor a leading `**/<file>` for a file that does not exist yet. So the escalation file (a new write) is permitted via the trailing-glob `Write(workorders/**)`, while tasks.md (an existing-file edit) works via `Edit(**/tasks.md)`. The CLAUDE.md/crucible.yaml denies are belt-and-suspenders — the legitimacy gate + CODEOWNERS are the real backstop.
